candidates.title: File:Entrance ticket to the 1975 Nobel Prize ceremony, front.jpg
candidates.description: Front side of an entrance ticket to the Nobel Prize ceremony 10 December 1975. The ticket is made out to Ann-Sophie Qvarnström. For this 75th anniversary of the prize, the ceremony was held at S:t Eriksmässan (now Stockholmsmässan or Stockholm International Fairs) in Älvsjö, Stockholm. The ticket is printed on thick pink paper. The ticket is scanned in Tuntorp, Brastad, Lysekil Municipality, Sweden.
candidates.creator: W.carter

candidates.title: File:Entrance ticket to the 1975 Nobel Prize ceremony, back.jpg
candidates.description: Back side of an entrance ticket to the Nobel Prize ceremony 10 December 1975. The ticket is made out to Ann-Sophie Qvarnström. For this 75th anniversary of the prize, the ceremony was held at S:t Eriksmässan (now Stockholmsmässan or Stockholm International Fairs) in Älvsjö, Stockholm. The ticket is printed on thick pink paper. The ticket is scanned in Tuntorp, Brastad, Lysekil Municipality, Sweden.
candidates.creator: W.carter

candidates.title: File:Anatomy lecture ticket 1860.jpg
candidates.description: Anatomy lecture ticket issued by the University of Glasgow for Practical Anatomy and Anatomical Demonstrations given by Allen Thomson MD. Ticket was issued 6 November 1860 and good for six months. Ticket was issued to Mr. James Macdonald. Ticket also confirms Macdonald attended demonstrations very regularly and performed dissection. Ticket is signed 23 April 1861 under the name Allen Thomson, although authenticity of this signature cannot be verified.

candidates.title: File:Receipt for Wine and Kegs Purchased by Meriwether Lewis for the Expedition to the West - NARA - 300351.jpg
candidates.description: Scope and content: In a secret message to Congress dated January 18, 1803, President Thomas Jefferson asked for $2,500 to explore lands west to the Pacific. Jefferson worked closely with Lewis, co-commander of the expedition, to ensure that he was well-prepared to anticipate the party's needs. While the party ran out of such luxuries as whiskey, tobacco, and salt, they had plenty of rifles, powder, paper, and ink. General notes: Box 560A. Exhibit History: "American Originals," December 1996 - December 1997, National Archives Rotunda, Washington, DC, Exhibit No. 624.0117.
candidates.creator: Unknown author Unknown author or not provided

candidates.title: File:Tambour desk (1790-1796).jpg
candidates.description: Tambour desk (Boston, 1790-1796) by John Seymour and Thomas Seymour . Mahogany with mahogany, satinwood and curly maple veneers. In this type of desk, introduced in 1790s, the bottom has drawers like a bureau, the top folds up, and there is a set of additional drawers behind the sliding screens.
candidates.creator: Votpuske

candidates.title: File:Palamuse kihelkonnakooli klassiruum.jpg
candidates.description: Palamuse Parish School's classroom. This 19th century classroom is now a part of a museum that bears the name of writer Oskar Luts , who studied there and wrote the book Kevade about his schooldays. It remains his best know work and the film adaption of the novel (that was also shot in Palamuse) is regarded as the best Estonian film in the 20th century.
candidates.creator: Rauno Kalda
